mission.title: clickhouse: Add new table engine UniqueMergeTree
mission.description:
### Changelog category (leave one):
- New Feature

### Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):
Add new table engine UniqueMergeTree.  Closes #41817.

Syntax:
```sql
CREATE TABLE [IF NOT EXISTS] [db.]table_name [ON CLUSTER cluster]
(
    name1 [type1] [DEFAULT|MATERIALIZED|ALIAS expr1],
    name2 [type2] [DEFAULT|MATERIALIZED|ALIAS expr2],
    ...
) ENGINE = UniqueMergeTree([ver])
[PARTITION BY expr]
[ORDER BY expr]
[PRIMARY KEY expr]
[UNIQUE

Query performance vs `ReplacingMergeTree`: 
- First generate ssb-flat, then `INSERT INTO SELECT * FROM ssb-flat` seperately into `ReplacingMergeTree` table and `UniqueMergeTree` table.
- Then execute the SSB query on these two tables.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
> I wasn't expecting the matview to perfectly work but I think the `LOGICAL_ERROR` should be avoided. 

Yes, currently, I did not consider the compatibility with `MV`, and it coundn't work well because we can not promise the exactly-once semantic for `MV`. 

And the `LOGIC_ERROR` occurs is because that in order to implement delete by insert, I add a special column for insert named `__delete_op`, when the column is exactly specify to 1(default 0), means we want to delete corresponding data, for example:

```sql
insert into uniq.landing(pk, __delete_op) values(1, 1);
```

which makes the `header` of UniqueMergeTreeSink has one more extra column named `__delete_op`.

it's a excellent engine for upsert 
hey @ucasfl, great job here, the engine alone works nice with upserts! I was trying to run some tests attaching a matview to the `UniqueMergeTree` engine to see if aggregates can be computed on to of it, and I've got a `LOGICAL_ERROR`.

I wasn't expecting the matview to perfectly work but I think the `LOGICAL_ERROR` should be avoided. Here you have the sql to replicate the test I was running:

> I wasn't expecting the matview to perfectly work but I think the `LOGICAL_ERROR` should be avoided. 

Yes, currently, I did not consider the compatibility with `MV`, and it coundn't work well because we can not promise the exactly-once semantic for `MV`. 

And the `LOGIC_ERROR` occurs is because that in order to implement delete by insert, I add a special column for insert named `__delete_op`, when the column is exactly specify to 1(default 0), means we want to delete corresponding data, for example:

which makes the `header` of UniqueMergeTreeSink has one more extra column named `__delete_op`.
does this ensure uniqueness across all shards?
> does this ensure uniqueness across all shards?

No, it just ensure uniqueness locally.
I did some test for Friday fun

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with clickhouse installed or the issue environment reproducible.
